_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Considering the relative costs and benefits of potential actions to choose the most appropriate one.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to apply risk assessment methodologies proportionally, matching the depth and rigor of risk analysis to the actual materiality and likelihood of the risk rather than applying uniform assessment intensity.
comments: Judgment and decision-making is foundational across WIDAI. Multiple Abilities express domain-specific judgment: RM-A-001 (proportional risk assessment), AG-A-001 (governance calibration), DQ-A-001 (impact-based prioritization), AB-A-002 (method selection). O*NET is the general capability; WIDAI operationalizes it extensively.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score is consistent with a Superset relationship where the O*NET element covers significantly broader ground than the WIDAI KSA. The shared conceptual territory is real but represents a fraction of the O*NET element's scope, compressing the similarity signal.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8563 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. The P > R pattern is atypical for a Superset relationship — it suggests the KSA may contain specificity that extends beyond the FDE's scope. This warrants attention during human review, though it may reflect domain-specific terminology in the KSA that the FDE covers conceptually without matching at the token level.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Entailment-dominant (98.17%) — the model detects that one description logically implies aspects of the other. This supports the Superset classification: the broader FDE's concepts encompass the narrower KSA's meaning.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.3345) is close to the STS cross-encoder (0.3674). The small gap (0.03) suggests the semantic relationship is largely capturable through independent encoding — the descriptions share enough surface-level semantic features that cross-attention adds only marginal signal.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (12.50% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
